rationale: Aristocratic constitutional monarchy with functioning Parliament; harsh repression of radicals (Six Acts) but routine peaceful succession and an effective wartime state.
Functioning constitutional monarchy passing major Reform Act (1832) and Corn Law repeal through orderly parliamentary politics; Chartism contained without state collapse.
Stable constitutional monarchy at the height of British power; competent bureaucracy, peaceful party alternation, no large-scale internal violence.
Mature constitutional democracy with effective state, expanding franchise and welfare politics, peaceful political competition.
Functioning wartime democracy under enormous strain; Easter Rising and Irish independence war localized; Westminster institutions held.
Routine democratic alternation, General Strike of 1926 contained, mass unemployment but no breakdown of governance.
Functioning wartime parliamentary democracy with coalition government; total mobilization without authoritarian collapse.
Stable postwar parliamentary democracy building NHS and welfare state through routine elections; 'Butskellite consensus' epitomizes effective governance.
Functioning democracy under stagflation and union militancy; Winter of Discontent and Northern Ireland Troubles strained but did not break the state.
Stable parliamentary democracy through contentious privatization and miners' strike; Falklands War a controlled foreign operation.
Stable parliamentary democracy with devolution to Scotland/Wales, Good Friday Agreement, and routine elections.
Functioning coalition government navigating austerity and 2014 Scottish referendum through routine constitutional politics.
Five PMs in eight years, Truss budget crisis (2022), record stagnation, deep partisan disorder; institutions held but frequent leadership chaos.
Routine post-election Labour government navigating fiscal constraints and Reform UK challenge; Westminster institutions functioning.
Fiscal collapse, fall of the Bastille, dissolution of absolute monarchy; brief constitutional monarchy experiment unstable but bounded transitional period.
Reign of Terror, mass executions, Vendée massacres, government coup cycle, civil war, abolition of feudalism in revolutionary chaos.
Centralized authoritarian state with Code Napoleon and effective bureaucracy; constant warfare but consistently effective domestic governance.
Restored Bourbon constitutional monarchy with limited charter; Ultra-royalist tensions but functioning state.
Bourgeois constitutional monarchy; narrow franchise but functioning industrial economy and stable governance until 1848.
Brief republic with universal male suffrage interrupted by June Days insurrection; political instability culminating in Louis-Napoleon's coup.
Authoritarian-modernizing empire with effective administration, Haussmann reconstruction; Franco-Prussian defeat ended it but governance was competent.
Born of Paris Commune bloodshed; Dreyfus Affair polarization contained within functioning parliamentary republic with effective secular reforms.
Functioning democracy with rapid government turnover (interwar), Popular Front polarization; institutions held until 1940 foreign-occupation collapse.
Collaborationist authoritarian regime under foreign occupation, Jewish deportations, severe wartime dysfunction; not state-directed mass killing on Nazi scale.
Chronic instability with 21 PMs in 12 years, ending in Algerian crisis collapse; functioning rebuilt economy but governance fragmented.
Stable presidential republic; May 1968 contained without collapse; effective dirigiste economic governance.
Routine presidential democracy with cohabitations; tournant de la rigueur, Maastricht ratified, normal political competition.
Stable democracy navigating euro transition, banlieue riots, EU constitution rejection; functioning institutions throughout.
Functioning democracy through Charlie Hebdo and Bataclan attacks; low approval but routine governance.
Gilets jaunes, pension protests, 2024 snap-election fragmentation, deep political polarization; institutions functioning but high contention.
Effective Stein-Hardenberg reforms after Jena defeat; competent state-building under wartime stress.
Metternich's conservative order with effective administrative state, censorship and surveillance but routine governance and no major violence.
Frankfurt Parliament collapse, military reconquest of revolutionary cities, brief constitutional disorder bounded to two years before Prussian state restored order.
Highly effective Prussian/German state under Bismarck — 'blood and iron' unification, pioneering social insurance, Kulturkampf managed without breakdown.
Effective imperial state with mass SPD politics, rapid industrialization, Weltpolitik; rising tensions but functioning institutions.
Hindenburg-Ludendorff military dictatorship, severe home-front deprivation, ending in revolution and Kaiser's abdication.
Hyperinflation 1923, paramilitary street violence, political assassinations, partisan fragmentation, presidential emergency rule, Nazi/Communist insurgency by 1932.
Totalitarian racial state, Holocaust, world war of aggression, complete destruction of democratic institutions, genocidal mass murder.
Postwar zone of occupation under Allied military government effectively rebuilding state from ruins, denazification, Cold War split looming.
Exceptionally stable postwar democracy, Wirtschaftswunder, strong constitutional protections, effective consensus governance.
Soviet-backed authoritarian state with Stasi surveillance and ideological conformity; long stable Soviet-bloc governance with economic stagnation.
Stable reunified democracy absorbing East Germany, anchoring Maastricht, effective Hartz/Agenda 2010 reforms through routine politics.
Stable consensus democracy navigating financial, eurozone, refugee crises through grand coalitions; high institutional capacity.
Traffic-light coalition collapse, AfD surge, energy shock and Zeitenwende, but routine 2025 election and CDU return; institutions functioning.
Functioning autocracy with serfdom intact, oscillating reform/reaction, effective Napoleonic-era resistance; stable imperial governance.
Repressive but effective autocracy, Decembrist revolt crushed, censorship and military rule, stable serfdom-based governance.
Effective top-down reforms — emancipation, zemstva, judiciary; some terrorism but functioning state.
Reactionary autocracy with Russification, pogroms, rapid industrialization under Witte; effective if illiberal state.
1905 Revolution forces Duma, WWI catastrophe, regime collapse by 1917; severe instability across the period.
Civil war 1918-21 (millions dead), war communism collapse, mass terror, 1921 famine, then NEP stabilization 1921-28; severe but partial bundled period.
Forced collectivization, Holodomor (millions dead), Great Purges, Gulag system, but state was ferociously operational; not collapsed despite massive predation.
Functional Soviet state, partial de-Stalinization, space race, Hungary intervention but no internal collapse.
Stable but stagnant Soviet state, dissident movement contained, Afghanistan war and Prague Spring suppression external; internal governance functional.
Glasnost/perestroika unleashing centrifugal forces, ethnic violence in periphery, economic collapse, USSR dissolution by 1991.
Shock-therapy collapse, oligarchic asset stripping, 1993 constitutional crisis (tanks shell parliament), Chechen wars, 1998 financial collapse — genuinely chaotic state failure.
Managed-democracy authoritarianism, oligarch-taming, Khodorkovsky imprisoned, effective restoration of state capacity; stable if illiberal regime.
Continued managed-democracy authoritarianism with Bolotnaya protests; stable if illiberal state.
Crimea annexation, Donbas war, Navalny suppression, Memorial closure, anti-LGBT laws; authoritarian consolidation but state operational.
Wartime mobilization state, Wagner mutiny, sanctions, draft, criminalization of dissent, mass emigration — significant dysfunction but state operational.

Ferdinand VII's absolutism reversal, Riego revolt, Carlist succession crisis with civil violence; functional but oscillating state.
Moderate liberal consolidation through pronunciamientos, desamortización, slow modernization; coup-prone but functional.

Intense polarization, military and clerical opposition, 1934 Asturias revolt; democratic state functioned until 1936 civil war outbreak.

Financial crisis, 2017 Catalan independence crisis, Indignados protests, two-party fragmentation; institutions held throughout.
